canonical_definition: The phenomenon where an auto-regressive model, after learning a forward relationship such as 'A is B', fails to generalize to the inverse relationship 'B is A' without explicit training on it.
papers: The Factorization Curse: Which Tokens You Predict Underlie the Reversal Curse and More
Towards a Theoretical Understanding of the 'Reversal Curse' via Training Dynamics

definitions.text: The phenomenon where an auto-regressive model, after learning a forward relationship such as 'A is B', fails to generalize to the inverse relationship 'B is A' without explicit training on it.
definitions.paper: Towards a Theoretical Understanding of the 'Reversal Curse' via Training Dynamics

relationships.paper: The Reversal Curse: LLMs trained on “A is B” fail to learn “B is A”
